meta.notes: Single-pass: built from PROMPT.md only, with no PROMPT-REVIEW.md self-review pass — so it did not get the 'second chance' every two-pass run gets. Treat comparisons with two-pass runs accordingly. Cost and token counts are omitted because this run produced no model-runner log; they are unknown, not zero. Prompt used: the 8-scored-category rubric, including the explicit out-of-scope list.

metrics.name: create-card-id
metrics.target: create response returns the new id

metrics.name: create-tx-id
metrics.target: create response returns the new id

metrics.name: unit-tests
metrics.target: unit tests for the business rules

metrics.observed: 72 % of 543 coverable lines (1 report(s)); 674 generated/composition-root line(s) excluded
metrics.target: line coverage >=60% (on the code that matters)

categories.name: Architecture & Design
notes: PROXY: layering, dependency direction, class size and unrequested machinery are all scored automatically from the Roslyn AST.

metrics.name: dependency-direction
metrics.target: domain does not reference infrastructure (Roslyn usings)

metrics.name: no-gold-plating
metrics.target: no machinery the brief ruled out (YAGNI)

categories.name: Persistence & Database
notes: PROXY: schema shape (migrations, FKs, indexes) is scored automatically from Roslyn; the schema is then exercised end to end by the live contract oracle.

notes: Live check (deep, harness): a real transaction event must land on the 'transactions' topic keyed by id â€” folded in from the harness kafka-check consumer.

categories.name: Security
notes: PROXY: scored automatically from the PCI checks (Luhn PAN / CVV / track / PIN over the Roslyn AST), gitleaks and the NuGet vulnerability graph.

metrics.target: no PAN (Luhn-valid card) embedded in code/config

metrics.name: no-stacktrace-leak
metrics.target: errors don't leak stack traces / internals
